commentary: Auction offerings totalled 5.0 M/Kgs, marginally below the previous week's 5.4 M/Kgs. Overall quality was mostly similar to last Demand: Fair demand. Liquoring Teas: Western High Grown BOP/BOPF's - Teas in the higher price bracket were irregular and sluggish partially following qualitative variances. Lighter/thinner liquoring teas notwithstanding an improved level of brightness failed to realise its value. Tea for price category, which at the commencement of the sale remained sluggish, gathered momentum as the sale progressed and sold around last week's levels with select coloury invoices appreciating by Rs. 20 per kg and more. Nuwara Eliyas - Limited interest and prices were barely maintained at its previous levels. Uda Pussellawas - Recorded a marginal appreciation in prices selectively though the price structure still continued at the lower end of the market. Uva - BOP's weakened by Rs. 20-40 per kg, whilst the BOPF's sold around last week's levels. High & Medium CTC teas - BP1's had hardly any offerings, whilst the PF1's continued to sell around last. Low Grown CTC teas - BP1's continued to maintain its previous momentum and sold at premium levels ranging between Rs. 1,300-1,400 per kg. Corresponding PF1's too continued to sell well recording prices ranging between Rs. 1,350-1,450 per kg and could be considered attractive in the current market context. commentary: The 50th and final sale of the year was conducted today (30 December 2026) following the re-scheduling of sales due to Cyclone Ditwah in late November. On account of a one-day Auction, offerings were restricted to 4.2 M/Kgs, of which Ex-Estate offerings comprised of 0.76 M/Kgs. Overall, a bearish market sentiment perhaps following the fewer number of working days from the previous week's auction. Quality of Ex-Estate offerings were barely maintained. Overall, a sharper decline for teas in the higher price bracket. Western BOP/BOPF's in the higher price bracket declined by up to Rs. 100 per kg and more for select high-priced teas. Most other teas on offer recorded a Rs. 20-40 per kg decline over the previous week's prices. The only exception would the Western High Grown 'BOP Special's' and 'PEK's' which were limited on offer and appreciated by up to Rs. 100 per kg. Low Growns totalled approximately 1.69 M/Kgs. Leafy, Semi-Leafy, Tippy and Premium categories met with fair demand. In the Leafy and Semi-Leafy catalogues, select BOP1's were firm, whilst the Best and Below Best together with the clean/bolder varieties were easier. Teas at the lower end maintained. High-priced OP1's were firm, whilst the Best, Below Best and teas at the bottom appreciated. OP's, in general, were lower. High-priced OPA's were easier, whilst the balance were irregular following quality. A few select Best PEK's appreciated on special inquiry, whilst the balance together with the Best were easier.
